commit.message:
E2e cli for cross-sdk tests (#1122)

* Add e2e-cli and e2e-tests workflow

Node-based CLI that copies SDK upload/error-handling logic for e2e
testing against mock server. Includes E2E_TEST_SUITES for selective
test execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add .gitignore for e2e-cli build artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Separate cdnHost from apiHost in e2e-cli, add README

Make apiHost and cdnHost optional in the CLI input type contract.
Add e2e-cli README documenting the input/output format and parameters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rewrite e2e-cli to run real SDK pipeline with Node.js stubs

Replace copied SDK functions with real imports from the SDK source.
Events now flow through the full pipeline: SegmentClient → Timeline →
SegmentDestination (batch chunking) → QueueFlushingPlugin (queue) →
uploadEvents HTTP POST.

Three minimal stubs replace React Native runtime dependencies:
- react-native: mocks AppState, NativeModules, Platform
- @segment/sovran-react-native: re-exports real store.ts + bridge.ts,
  bypassing the RN bridge entry point
- react-native-get-random-values: no-op (Node.js has native crypto)

Uses esbuild to bundle CLI + SDK source + stubs into dist/cli.js.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add E2E_TESTS_TOKEN for private sdk-e2e-tests repo checkout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add e2e-config.json and run-e2e.sh for local E2E testing

Per-SDK config and convenience script for the generic test runner
in sdk-e2e-tests. Run ./e2e-cli/run-e2e.sh to build and test locally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update CI workflow to use run-tests.sh from sdk-e2e-tests

Replace hardcoded env vars and direct npm test call with
./scripts/run-tests.sh which reads e2e-config.json for test
configuration. This ensures CI uses the same config as local runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add e2e-cli/ to .eslintignore

The e2e-cli directory has its own tsconfig and is not part of the
SDK's lint setup. Excluding it prevents ESLint parsing errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix e2e-cli review comments: types, field mapping, sovran stub

- Align types with sdk-e2e-tests (AnalyticsEvent, EventSequence,
  CLIConfig, CLIOutput with sentBatches)
- Fix screen to use evt.name instead of evt.event
- Fix group to use evt.groupId instead of evt.event
- Replace non-null assertions with validation errors
- Add page event type (throws: not supported on mobile)
- Add default case for unknown event types
- Remove AsyncStoragePersistor export from sovran stub (pulls in
  @react-native-async-storage which breaks Node.js)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Map page events to screen in e2e-cli for consistency

Mobile SDKs don't have a page method. Kotlin and Swift CLIs already
map page → screen silently. Match that behavior here instead of
throwing an error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove CLI-side validation, let SDK handle invalid input

The CLI should be a thin pass-through so e2e tests observe actual
SDK behavior for missing fields, not CLI pre-validation errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Validate and warn on invalid events, skip instead of crashing

Use team-suggested validation pattern: warn on missing/invalid
required fields and skip the event, validate optional fields and
proceed without them if invalid, catch per-event errors so one
bad event doesn't fail the entire sequence.

commit.message:
fix(frontend): fix blank screen on summary page after FastAPI migration

Three bugs caused a white crash screen on /summary/:id:

1. SummaryView: isLoading initialised to false, so Summary rendered
   immediately with an empty {} object, calling formatDate(undefined)
   which throws RangeError — fixed by initialising isLoading to true
2. SummaryView: currentSummary.length === 0 always false on an object
   (undefined !== 0) — fixed with !currentSummary.id guard
3. Field name mismatch: Flask returned `text`, FastAPI returns `content`
   — updated SummaryView, Summary, and TypingSummary to use `content`
   (Summary retains `?? summary.text` fallback for safety)
4. formatDate: added null guard to prevent RangeError on invalid dates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix cloud_agent merge backend: comment on PR instead of creating separate issue (#597)

* Initial plan

* fix: cloud_agent merge backend now comments on PR instead of creating new issue

The cloud agent backend for merge conflict resolution was creating a new
GitHub issue and assigning the Copilot coding agent to it. However, the
agent works on its own branch and cannot perform a proper git merge across
branches, resulting in PRs with 0 actual changes.

This changes the cloud_agent backend to post a @copilot comment directly
on the conflicting PR, asking the agent to reapply the changes on top of
the latest base branch. This approach works because the agent operates
within the PR context rather than on a separate branch.

Changes:
- core/src/state.rs: Add `comment_only` field to PendingMergeIssue
- cli/src/merge.rs: resolve_via_cloud_agent now uses comment_on_pr
- cli/src/merge.rs: promote_pending_merge_issues handles comment-only
  entries by checking PR conflict status
- Updated and added tests for the new behavior

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix unwrap_or for mergeable check, improve docs

commit.message:
Replace SendKeys with ConPTY for focus-free automation

- New lib/conpty.ps1: C# ConPTY wrapper providing headless interactive PTY
- Rewritten lib/executor.ps1: writes commands via pipes instead of SendKeys
- No window focus required — works in background without Dev Box tab active
- Task/done files moved to %TEMP%\sarma\ (keeps repo clean)
- Executor now appends completion signal to all prompts (removed from sarma.ps1/worker)
- KeepAlive mode: closes headless PTY, opens visible terminal with --resume
